externalfield.help: Field given in x y z components

ecp.help: Effective Core Potentials for DFT Calculations

functional.help: Exchange correlation functional used. You can also specify an exchange and a correlation functional

dipole_spacing.help: Spacing for splitting multipoles into charges

temporary_file.help: Filename for temporary files without extension

convergence_tightness.help: How accurate the dft self-consistency has to be

cleanup.help: files to remove after the calculation. Specify the fileextensions

initial_guess.help: Method to use to make initial guess, independent(electrons) or atom(densities) or previous calculation keyword orbfile

orca.help: orca specific keywords can be added here, where the xml tag corresponds to the option and the value to the option's value

xtpdft.help: xtpdft specific keywords can be added here

activeatoms.help: Indices of atoms in active region

threshold.help: Mulliken population above which orbital is considered in the active region

levelshift.help: Levelshift energy for the projection operator

truncate_basis.help: Truncate the basis-set to active atoms only

truncation_threshold.help: Threshold to convert an inactive atom to a border atom

fock_matrix_reset.help: how often the fock matrix is reset

compute_forces.help: Compute analytic ground-state DFT nuclear forces (RI, and hybrid-exchange for UKS) and store them in the orb (HDF5) file. Adds real cost to every converged SCF, so this is opt-in.

cdft.help: Constrained DFT (CDFT): a Hirshfeld-based charge constraint on a fragment of the molecule, via an outer, warm-started Lagrange-multiplier loop around the ordinary UKS SCF (Wu-Van Voorhis approach). Opt-in via cdft.enabled -- has no effect on any calculation unless explicitly enabled, matching compute_forces's own opt-in convention just above.

enabled.help: Enable a CDFT charge constraint on this calculation

indices.help: Indices of atoms in the constrained fragment (0-based), using the same range syntax already used for diabatization.xml's own fragment indices, e.g. '1 3 13:17'

charge.help: Target charge on the fragment, RELATIVE to its neutral reference state (the sum of the fragment atoms' own nuclear charges) -- e.g. +1.0 means one electron REMOVED from the fragment (a cation), -1.0 means one electron ADDED (an anion). Converted internally to an absolute target electron count once, at setup time.

initial_lambda.help: Initial guess for the Lagrange multiplier lambda, used as the center of the outer loop's own bisection bracket

population_tolerance.help: Outer-loop convergence criterion: the CDFT calculation is considered converged once the achieved fragment population is within this many electrons of the target

max_iterations.help: Maximum number of outer (Lagrange-multiplier, bisection) iterations -- each one is a full, warm-started inner SCF

energy.help: DeltaE at which calculation is converged

method.help: Main method to use for convergence accelertation

DIIS_start.help: DIIS error at which DIIS takes over

ADIIS_start.help: DIIS error at which ADIIS takes over

DIIS_length.help: old hamiltonians to keep in history

DIIS_maxout.help: if true remove the maximum element in the history if DIIS_length is exceeded.Otherwise, the oldest element is removed.

levelshift_end.help: DIIS error at levelshifting is disabled

mixing.help: mixing parameter for linear mixing of density matrices
